name: Nauru
avoid: November-February is hotter, wetter and stickier, so the exposed Topside loop, Command Ridge and beach time feel much rougher than in the drier March-October window.
Accommodation is limited and often arranged by email or phone, so do not lock flights until your room and airport transfer are confirmed.
Nauru is tiny but services are thin: carry AUD cash, refill water early and expect informal guide, bike and airport-transfer arrangements rather than app-based bookings.
stopoverNote: Nauru is best planned as a focused 1-2 day stop: one day for Yaren-side WWII and cave sights, then one more for Anibare Bay, Buada Lagoon and the phosphate-scarred Topside loop.

cities.name: Yaren District
cities.notes: Yaren is the practical arrival base around the airport and government precinct, close to Command Ridge, Moqua Well and short island-loop drives in every direction.

cities.name: Anibare Bay
cities.notes: Anibare Bay is Nauru's prettiest coastal stretch, pairing Menen-side stays with the east-coast beach, nearby Buada Lagoon detours and the stark phosphate pinnacles inland.

itinerary.sim: Digicel Nauru is the only practical tourist SIM; a starter prepaid SIM commonly costs about AUD 10, so buy it at the airport or in town and download offline maps before you head into Topside or the east-coast beaches.

Yaren District.name: Yaren District
Yaren District.note: One compact Yaren day is enough for arrival logistics, the WWII ridge, the freshwater cave stop and a first complete sense of how tiny Nauru really is.

days.theme: Airport Arrival, Command Ridge Relics and the Moqua Well Stop

activities.name: Digicel Nauru prepaid SIM setup
activities.tip: A basic Digicel starter SIM is commonly about AUD 10; buy it on arrival so you can message your host and keep offline maps synced.

activities.name: Rental car for the island loop
activities.tip: Recent online car-hire pricing for Nauru commonly lands around AUD 73-146 per day; reserve through your hotel or ahead online because availability is thin.

activities.name: Command Ridge WWII guns, bunkers and lookout
activities.tip: There is no standard ticket for the ridge itself; go early or late for softer light and less heat on the exposed high point.

activities.name: Moqua Well freshwater cave stop
activities.tip: There is no formal entry booth, but travelers often leave a small AUD 10-20 thank-you if a local helps with access or context around the well.

activities.name: Parliament precinct and airport-side walk in Yaren

Anibare Bay.name: Anibare Bay
Anibare Bay.note: The second day is the scenic Nauru loop: one beach-and-lagoon stop, one Topside phosphate run and one slow meal with an ocean view before departure.

days.theme: Phosphate Pinnacles, Buada Lagoon and the East-Coast Bay

activities.name: Topside phosphate pinnacles self-drive circuit
activities.tip: There is no official site ticket, so the real cost is your rental-car day; Nauru's limestone pinnacles are easiest as part of a slow self-drive or hotel-arranged island circuit.

activities.name: Buada Lagoon walk and bird-watching stop
activities.tip: Buada Lagoon is a public natural stop with no entry fee; visit in the morning if you want the coolest loop and the greenest photos.

activities.name: Command Ridge revisit or full island perimeter drive by bicycle
activities.tip: Walking costs nothing and some hosts can sort simple bike hire around AUD 10-20 a day if you want to pedal sections of the 21-sq-km island.

activities.name: Anibare Bay beach walk and Anibare Boat Harbour lookout

connections.method: Short hotel pickup, local lift or a quick walk if you are traveling very light and staying near the airport side.
Continue the island loop by rental car, or bike the sealed coastal road if conditions are cool enough and your guesthouse can arrange a cycle.
A 15-20 minute drive back along the ring road; reconfirm the transfer time because flights are infrequent and check-in is simple but not frequent enough to risk delays.
connections.cost: Included in transfer or car hire

extras: Nauru is only about 21 sq km, so the island works best when you think in one clockwise or anticlockwise loop rather than separate day trips.
AUD cash is still the safest way to handle hotel meals, guide tips and informal transport because card acceptance is inconsistent outside the better-known hotels.
The island's key sights are exposed rather than sheltered, so carry water, a hat and reef-safe sun protection even if the total driving distance looks tiny.
If you only have one day, combine Command Ridge, Moqua Well, Buada Lagoon and Anibare Bay in the same rental-car loop and skip trying to over-structure it.
